raw_consumer_statement:
background : A scam company calling it XXXX XXXX  got my American Express card number by posing as a XXXX agent. In my previous compliant with cfpb, they had charged {$1100.00} on the card and AMEX initially resolved my dispute in their favor ( and, presumably keeping them as a trusted vendor ). After cfpb got involved Amex eventually reversed the charge. 

There were many layers to this scam. They would make payments to XXXX with stolen credit cards to cover their tracks. 

In my case, once the theft was exposed they attempted to pay my XXXX policy with someone elses card. I alerted XXXX to this and i thought we were done. 

On XX/XX/XXXX two charges appeared on my account for {$220.00} and {$190.00} paid to XXXX XXXX. 

I reached out to XXXX and they confirmed that those charges were not applied to my policy. The XXXX rep said that when AMEX investigates they ( XXXX ) will tell them the charge is not applied to the card-holders policy.

I disputed the charge with AMEX. They have closed the dispute and determined that i am responsible for the charge.

They seem uninterested in addressing the fraud.

I have since reported the card lost.

Attached screenshot of my AMEX charges, One legitimate charge {$460.00} to XXXX for my auto insurance. The other two XXXX charges were not made by me.
